concerns.text: T022 ('Implement multi-seed sweep... to calculate cosine similarity') is marked [P] (parallel-safe). However, calculating cosine similarity for stability (SC-004) requires aggregating results from multiple independent NMF runs. A single parallel task cannot perform this aggregation without shared state or a subsequent sequential step. The task conflates the parallel execution of seeds with the sequential aggregation logic, violating the [P] constraint.

T030 implements a 'time-shuffled baseline' (random null), but FR-009 explicitly requires a 'null model (linear mixing of behavior)'. T032 generates the report. There is no task to implement the specific 'linear mixing' algorithm required by FR-009. The consumer (T032) expects an artifact (linear mixing results) that the producer (T030) does not generate, creating a missing dependency in the data flow.

T013 implements interpolation for missing data but lacks a distinct task or explicit logic branch to raise `MemoryExceededError` or a specific data-error when missing rate > 5% (per FR-002 Edge Cases). The consumer (T014 deconvolution) assumes valid input, but the producer (T013) does not explicitly define the failure path for the >5% case, risking silent pipeline continuation with invalid data.

T024 implements 'alignment of extracted component weights' but does not include the calculation of the 'alignment error' metric required by SC-005 (≤ 1 frame). The success criterion (SC-005) is a measurable outcome, but no task exists to produce the 'alignment error' artifact. The validation step (Phase 3) cannot verify SC-005 because the metric is never computed.
